name: Las Burbonas Trail
name_original: Las Tres Burbonas por el río Bayas — circular trailrunnig por el Parque Natural de Gorbeia
description: This 8.3-mile (13.3 km) difficult trailrunning route through Gorbeia Natural Park explores the ancient beech forests of Las Tres Burbonas.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Zuya/Zuia are primarily located within and around the Gorbea Natural Park, the largest natural reserve in the Basque Country. This region is characterized by its diverse terrain, including gentle, rolling pastures, extensive beech and oak forests, and significant geological formations like karst land. Runners can explore a well-developed network of trails that traverse varied landscapes, from river valleys to the foothills of Mount Gorbea. Best jogging routes around Zuya/Zuia The most popular jogging route is Las Burbonas Trail, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 46 minutes to complete. This route leads through ancient beech forests and offers a moderate challenge. Another top favourite among local runners is Gorbea Summit Cross – Gorbea Summit loop from Gorbeialdea, a difficult 10.6 miles (17.1 km) path. This route features a significant ascent to the iconic Gorbea Summit, offering expansive views. Local runners also love the Gorbea Summit Cross – Gorbea Summit loop from Zuia, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail leading through the Gorbea Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Jogging around Zuya/Zuia is defined by dense forests, rolling hills, and mountain peaks within an extensive trail network. The region offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Zuya/Zuia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Zuya/Zuia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Zuya/Zuia?
A: There are over 25 running routes around Zuya/Zuia, primarily located within and around the Gorbea Natural Park. These trails offer a diverse range of experiences for runners.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the running trails in Zuya/Zuia?
A: The running trails in Zuya/Zuia cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 9 moderate routes and 19 difficult routes, many of which involve significant elevation gain as they ascend towards Mount Gorbea. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058965026/las-burbonas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Burbonas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24569885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Martín (Jugo) – Echevarría Bakery loop from Murgia&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate option.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths in the Zuya/Zuia area?
A: While many routes in the Gorbea Natural Park are challenging, there are moderate options that families with older children or those looking for a less strenuous run might enjoy. The region's extensive trail network includes paths through gentle pastures and forests, which can be suitable for a family outing. Always check the route details for distance and elevation before heading out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Zuya/Zuia?
A: The Gorbea Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Be mindful of other trail users and ensure your dog is well-behaved. Always check specific park regulations or route descriptions for any restrictions.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while running in Zuya/Zuia?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter impressive geological formations like karst land and extensive beech and oak forests. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3009759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uguzpe Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2824175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nervión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The iconic Gorbea Summit Cross is also a significant landmark for those tackling the more challenging mountain routes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Zuya/Zuia?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Zuya/Zuia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6976594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit Cross – Gorbea Summit loop from Gorbeialdea&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10520670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit Cross – Gorbea Summit loop from Zuia&lt;/a&gt;. These circular paths are convenient for exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Zuya/Zuia?
A: The Gorbea Natural Park offers beautiful scenery year-round. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant running conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher parts of Mount Gorbea, requiring appropriate gear and caution. Always check the local weather forecast before your run.

Q: Where can I park when accessing running trails in Zuya/Zuia?
A: Many trailheads within the Gorbea Natural Park and around Zuya/Zuia offer designated parking areas. For routes starting near Murgia, you can often find parking facilities. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Zuya/Zuia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from dense forests to rolling hills and mountain peaks, and the extensive network of trails that allow for varied experiences. The natural beauty of the Gorbea Natural Park is a consistent highlight.

Q: Are there any caves I can explore near the running routes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its caves. While not directly on running paths, you can find several notable caves nearby, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2652013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Goros Caves&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mairulegorreta&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3053569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Supelegor&lt;/a&gt;. These offer an interesting diversion for those looking to explore the geological wonders of the area.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Zuya/Zuia?
A: Yes, Zuya/Zuia offers several challenging long-distance running trails, particularly those ascending Mount Gorbea. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24694540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit Cross – Gorbea Summit loop from Murgia&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 23.4 km route with over 900 meters of elevation gain, ideal for experienced runners seeking a significant challenge.
tips: The easternmost summit of the Bourbon triplets.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Las Tres Burbonas trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied terrain, including paths (senda), trails (sendero), mountain trails (sendero de montaña), and short sections of road. You'll find tracks leading into a beautiful beech forest and an ascent. In good weather, it's possible to cross directly through the Bayas River. The entire route is well-marked with circular yellow signs on posts and trees.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this trail as 'difficult' for jogging. While some sources describe it as 'relatively simple' for hiking in good weather and potentially suitable for families, the varied terrain and ascent mean it's best suited for those with some trail running or hiking experience. Conditions can become challenging with mud in winter.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The most striking feature is the extensive and beautiful beech forest that covers the 'Tres Burbonas' (three peaks). You'll pass through the Western Burbona Forest. While the views from the summits like Burbona Central Summit and Burbona East are largely obstructed by the dense beech trees, the forest itself provides a captivating natural environment. The opportunity to traverse the Bayas River in good weather is also a unique element.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within Gorbeia Natural Park. While specific parking details for this exact trailhead aren't provided, generally, parking is available at designated areas within the park. It's advisable to check local park information or maps for the most convenient starting point and parking facilities.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable in favorable weather conditions. Spring and autumn are often ideal for trail running, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage. During winter, adverse weather and mud can make the trail more challenging, though it's still suitable for trail running/walking rather than mountain biking. The option to cross the Bayas River is best in good weather.

faqs.question: Is the Las Tres Burbonas trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, trails within Gorbeia Natural Park are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Please also ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for Gorbeia Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within Gorbeia Natural Park for activities like hiking or trail running. However, it's always good practice to check the official Gorbeia Natural Park website for any updated regulations or specific requirements, especially for larger groups or organized events.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You will find sections that overlap with the Artzaintza naturbidea, Euskal Herriko ibilaldia, Camino de Aldarro a Burbona, Senda a Astaiz, Senda a Burbona Central, Puente de Aldarro, and Puente de Igaz.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for trail running on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for river crossings, wear appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. Dress in layers suitable for the weather conditions, as temperatures can change. Bring enough water, especially since there's no mention of water sources on the trail itself. A small backpack with snacks, a first-aid kit, and navigation tools (like the komoot app) is also recommended.

faqs.question: Is there an insider tip for experiencing the Bayas River crossing?
faqs.answer: Yes, a unique aspect of this trail is the possibility of crossing directly through the Bayas River itself. This is best done in favorable weather conditions when the water levels are lower. It adds an engaging element to the adventure, but always assess the conditions carefully before attempting to cross.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this 13.3 km circular trail typically takes around 1 hour and 46 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.
